Wed May 13, 2026: Energy Transfer's Growth Traced to Kelcy Warren's Leadership Legacy

Energy Transfer LP ranks among the largest publicly traded energy companies in the United States, moving natural gas, natural gas liquids, crude oil, and refined products through a network that spans the country. On 13th May 2026, it became clear that this scale didn't happen by accident. The executive team behind Energy Transfer reflects decades of accumulated experience, and the culture traces directly back to the company's founder.

Kelcy Warren co-founded Energy Transfer in 1996 with roughly 200 miles of natural gas pipeline in East Texas. Since then, it's grown into a national midstream enterprise handling natural gas, natural gas liquids, crude oil, and refined products. As Executive Chairman and Chairman of the Board, Warren has been in the energy sector for more than 45 years, earning accolades that include membership in the Horatio Alger Association of Distinguished Americans and induction into the Texas Business Hall of Fame. His philanthropic efforts, including a $12 million donation to the University of Texas-Arlington, underscore his commitment to community engagement.

Day-to-day operations at Energy Transfer fall to co-CEOs Tom Long and Mackie McCrea, both of whom have spent years learning the intricacies of the company. Long joined through the 2015 merger with Regency GP LLC and has held various positions, including Group CFO. McCrea's tenure at Energy Transfer dates back to 1997, and he has advanced through several key roles leading to his current position as co-CEO. Their deep-rooted experience reflects a managerial model designed to effectively handle the scale of the operations.

The company's institutional depth extends into its finance and legal functions, which are led by long-standing executives. Dylan Bramhall, Group Chief Financial Officer, has been with Energy Transfer since 2006 and oversees critical financial operations. Jim Wright, Executive Vice President, General Counsel, and Chief Compliance Officer, joined the company's legal team in 2005. The longevity and expertise of these leaders contribute to the organizational stability and strategic direction of Energy Transfer.

The consistency across the executive bench at Energy Transfer is indicative of the organizational culture that Kelcy Warren has built over nearly three decades. This leadership framework has positioned Energy Transfer as a major force in U.S. energy infrastructure, reflecting not only the company's growth but also its commitment to excellence in the energy sector.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 296 words

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Energy Transfer LP Boosts Quarterly Cash Distribution, Sparking Investor Interest

Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET) has announced an increase in its quarterly cash distribution to $0.3375 per common unit, amounting to an annualized rate of $1.35. This announcement comes as part of the company's first-quarter results for the year, which ended on the 31st of March, 2026.

This new distribution rate represents an increase of over 3 percent compared to the same quarter in the previous year, highlighting Energy Transfer's commitment to returning value to its unitholders. The distribution will be paid on the 20th of May, 2026, to unitholders of record as of the close of business on the 8th of May 2026.

In conjunction with this announcement, Energy Transfer LP has scheduled the release of its earnings report for the first quarter of 2026 on the 5th of May 2026, before the market opens. A conference call will follow at 8:00 a.m. Central Time, where the company's management will discuss the quarterly results and provide updates on its operations. Investors and analysts can access the call via a live webcast on the Energy Transfer website.

Energy Transfer LP stands as one of the largest operators in the energy sector in the United States, boasting a portfolio that includes approximately 140,000 miles of pipeline and related infrastructure. The company's operations span across 44 states and encompass various segments, including natural gas, crude oil, and natural gas liquids. This diversification positions Energy Transfer favorably in the market, allowing it to capitalize on growth opportunities within the energy landscape.

As the company continues to expand its operational capabilities, investors are keenly watching for further updates on distribution levels and overall company performance. Energy Transfer's strong cash distribution growth is expected to generate interest among income-seeking investors, reinforcing its reputation as a key player in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE AND INDEPENDENT RATINGS

5.1 Activities

Energy Transfer LP is a leading master limited partnership traded on the New York Stock Exchange, focused on midstream energy infrastructure in the United States. The company primarily engages in the gathering, processing, transportation, and storage of natural gas, natural gas liquids (NGLs), crude oil, and refined products through an extensive network of pipelines, terminals, and storage facilities. This integrated approach supports energy producers and consumers, enhancing supply chain efficiency across domestic and international markets. Among its fastest-growing segments are NGL fractionation and logistics, fueled by increasing demand from the petrochemical sector and export opportunities, as well as expansions in crude oil transportation infrastructure. Additionally, Energy Transfer is investing in renewable natural gas and low-carbon initiatives, positioning it for sustainable growth in a transitioning energy industry. With a strong operational footprint, the company plays a vital role in North America's energy supply. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

Energy Transfer LP (NYSE:ET) is a leading natural gas pipeline operator offering comprehensive midstream services including NGL pipeline services, crude oil transportation, and storage terminals. Recently, Energy Transfer LP announced an increase in its quarterly cash distribution, underlining its commitment to enhancing shareholder value through a boosted dividend policy. The Energy Transfer cash distribution per unit, including details like the distribution record date and payable date, was officially revealed in their press release distribution increase, demonstrating financial robustness and shareholder focus. Energy Transfer’s distributable cash flow (DCF) and distribution coverage ratio further highlight its strategic positioning in the market as it continues to deliver steady growth in Energy Transfer midstream distributions. With an expanded footprint in LPG distribution and a solid track record, investors can expect a consistent return from Energy Transfer's distribution yield, reinforced by diligent investor relations and a positive payout update for stakeholders.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

TXNM Energy Inc., a utility holding company operating in the southwestern United States, primarily focuses on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas. As a key player in the energy sector, the company serves residential, commercial, and industrial customers in New Mexico and Texas, emphasizing reliable and sustainable power solutions. Its core activities encompass regulated utility operations, including power plant management and infrastructure maintenance, alongside strategic investments in energy efficiency and grid modernization. The fastest-growing segments of TXNM Energy's business include renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. These initiatives have bolstered the company's portfolio, positioning it for long-term growth amid the transition to a low-carbon economy, while maintaining a strong commitment to operational excellence and customer service. It is the NYSE's 15th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Press Release article 2 of 5, Source: WEC, 86 words

December 04 2025: TXNM Energy quarterly dividends up 3.7% sequentially from previous quarter

TXNM Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 42.25c per share for Q4/2025; up 3.7% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 40.75c. The ex-dividend date is Friday, January 30, 2026 and the record date is Friday, January 30, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, February 13, 2026. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended December 04 were $1.63.

Press Release article 3 of 5, 296 words

September 30 2025: TXNM Energy: File SEC Form 10-Q - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-Q

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

The following Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations for TXNM is presented on a combined basis, including certain information applicable to PNM and TNMP. The MD&A for PNM and TNMP is presented as permitted by Form 10-Q General Instruction H(2). This report uses the term "Company" when discussing matters of common applicability to TXNM, PNM, and TNMP. A reference to a "Note" in this Item 2 refers to the accompanying Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements (Unaudited) included in Item 1, unless otherwise specified. Certain of the tables below may not appear visually accurate due to rounding.

MD&A FOR TXNM

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Overview

TXNM is a holding company with two regulated electric utilities, PNM and TNMP, serving approximately 839,000 residential, commercial, and industrial customers in New Mexico and Texas. TXNM strives to create a clean and bright energy future for customers, communities, and shareholders. TXNM's strategy and decision-making are focused on safely providing reliable, affordable, and environmentally responsible power built on a foundation of sustainability.

Recent Developments

Merger

On May 18, 2025, TXNM, Parent, and Merger Sub (both Parent and Merger Sub are affiliates of Blackstone Infrastructure) entered into the Merger Agreement pursuant to which Merger Sub will merge with and into TXNM, with TXNM surviving the Merger as a direct, wholly-owned subsidiary of Parent. Pursuant to the Merger Agreement, each issued and outstanding share of the common stock of TXNM (other than those listed in Note 17) at the Effective Time will be converted into the right to receive $61.25 in cash, without interest.

The proposed Merger has been unanimously approved by the Board.

Press Release article 4 of 5, Source: WEC, 78 words

September 25 2025: TXNM Energy quarterly dividends steady

TXNM Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 40.75c per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q2/2025) dividend of 40.75c. The ex-dividend date is Friday, October 24, 2025 and the record date is Friday, October 24, 2025 and it is payable on Friday, November 14. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended September 25 were $1.61.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: Company Website, 317 words

August 28 2025: TXNM Energy Shareholders Overwhelmingly Approve Acquisition by Blackstone Infrastructure

TXNM Energy (NYSE: TXNM) shareholders voted overwhelmingly to approve the agreement under which Blackstone Infrastructure will acquire TXNM Energy at a special shareholders meeting held earlier today. Under the terms of the proposed agreement, TXNM Energy shareholders will receive $61.25 in cash for each share of TXNM Energy common stock held at closing.

Of the shares voted, 99.6 percent voted to approve the proposed agreement, reflecting 88.2 percent of shares issued and outstanding on the record date. TXNM Energy will report the final vote results in a Current Report on Form 8-K to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Regulatory approvals from the New Mexico Public Regulation Commission, Public Utility Commission of Texas, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, Department of Justice (Hart Scott-Rodino Clearance), Nuclear Regulatory Commission and Federal Communications Commission are being pursued.

TXNM Energy continues to anticipate that the closing of the acquisition will occur in the second half of 2026, subject to the satisfaction or waiver of the customary closing conditions, including among other things, receipt of required state and federal regulatory approvals.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W0RJ-00000-00NRG Energy's Strategic Moves Signal Growth Potential in U.S. Energy Markets2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:47ZLexisNexisNRG Energy's Strategic Moves Signal Growth Potential in U.S. Energy Markets

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 07:16 EDT

NRG Energy, a leading independent power generator, continues to expand its footprint in the U.S. retail energy and renewables sectors amid rising demand for clean energy solutions. As of 11th May 2026, the company's strategic initiatives underscore its commitment to sustainability and reliability, making it an intriguing option for investors monitoring the energy sector.

Operating from its headquarters in Houston, Texas, NRG Energy holds a significant position in the U.S. power generation and retail energy markets. The company boasts a diverse portfolio that includes natural gas, coal, oil, nuclear, solar, and battery storage facilities, serving a wide range of residential, commercial, and industrial customers. With a focus on optimizing asset performance and customer engagement, NRG leverages its substantial generation capacity of over 16 GW to maintain a competitive edge.

Retail electricity sales are the primary revenue driver for NRG Energy, accounting for approximately 70% of total revenues in 2024. The company’s retail operations, which include brands like Reliant Energy, have thrived in deregulated markets such as Texas and Illinois. NRG's strategy includes long-term customer contracts and demand response programs that provide stability in earnings, especially amid fluctuating market conditions. Furthermore, the integration of renewables and battery storage into its operations aligns with the increasing demand for sustainable energy solutions.

As the U.S. power sector grapples with rising demand due to factors such as data centers and electrification, NRG Energy stands to benefit significantly. The company's strong presence in Texas positions it well to capitalize on projected capacity needs, with ERCOT forecasting an additional 50 GW required by 2030. While competitors like Vistra and Constellation Energy are also vying for market share, NRG’s extensive retail capabilities and innovative storage solutions set it apart in a fragmented industry.

For U.S. investors, NRG Energy offers a compelling opportunity to engage with the backbone of America's energy infrastructure. The company’s operations, particularly in high-growth regions, provide exposure to both traditional and renewable energy sectors. With a focus on maintaining reliable cash flows and strategic expansions, NRG Energy is an attractive option for those seeking long-term growth in the energy market, especially as the industry shifts towards a greener future.

April 23: NRG Energy quarterly dividends steady

NRG Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 47.50c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 47.50c. The ex-dividend date is Friday, May 01, 2026 and the record date is Friday, May 01, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, May 15. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended April 23 were $1.79.

March 04: NRG Energy substantial shareholder sells

NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) substantial shareholder Nanus David sold 16,129,269 shares worth $2,645,200,116 on March 04. The selling price was $164.0.

March 04: NRG Energy substantial shareholder sells

NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) substantial shareholder LS Power Equity Advisors, LLC sold 16,129,269 shares worth $2,645,200,116 on March 04. The selling price was $164.0.

SECTION 2 NRG ENERGY ACTIVITIES

Activities

NRG Energy, Inc., a leading integrated power company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in the generation, transmission, and retail supply of electricity across the United States. The company's core activities include operating a diverse portfolio of power plants fueled by natural gas, coal, nuclear, and renewables, while also providing energy services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers. NRG emphasizes innovative retail solutions, such as demand response programs and energy management tools, to enhance efficiency and customer engagement. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support for clean energy and market demand for sustainable options. Additionally, the company's distributed energy resources, including battery storage and microgrids, are gaining momentum, driven by advancements in technology and the shift toward decentralized power systems. This strategic focus positions NRG for continued growth in a competitive and evolving energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

12 May 2026 11:32 EDT

The updated model expands beyond traditional auto and energy assets by incorporating revenue streams such as insurance operations and Supercharging revenue, and it distinguishes the robotaxi opportunity from FSD software licensing. Potter suggests the robot and software components could be valued separately, with Optimus potentially surpassing the value of Tesla’s existing businesses combined, though formal long-term forecasts for these segments have yet to be published. Optimus remains nascent, with initial deployments focused on internal factory tasks before broader commercial use, and success will hinge on execution, regulatory approvals, and market acceptance of humanoid robotics.

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-3NN3-RSP0-R48S-00000-00AXENS S.A. - UPDATES FROM BASF SE AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKAXENS S.A. - UPDATES FROM BASF SE AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [3 Press Releases]

+ AXENS S.A. A NEW TEXTILE-TO-TEXTILE RECYCLING PROCESS VALIDATED ON AN INDUSTRIAL SCALE

Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN have completed a recycling loop for polyester textiles. Several tens of tons of post-consumer, polyester-rich, European textile wastes, sorted and prepared in France, have been processed in the Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN semi-industrial demonstration unit, located in Japan, to successfully produce the base monomer of a 100% recycled polyester. This step paves the way for circular polyester loops for the textile industry, in particular sportswear, home furnishings and the luxury sector.

Source: Company Website

+ PRAJ DEMONSTRATES INTEGRATED ETHANOL-TO-JET PATHWAY FOR SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL WITH AXENS JETANOL(TRADEMARK) TECHNOLOGY

30 January, 2026 - Praj Industries, a global leader in bioenergy, technology and sustainable solutions, has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) process utilizing Axens JetanolTM technology at its fully Integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre-marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low-carbon aviation fuels. The announcement was made by Mr. Atul Mulay, President - Bionergy at Praj Industries at Wings India 2026, India's premier aviation industry platform in the presence of industry leaders, policymakers, and key stakeholders from the global aviation ecosystem.

The Integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Hon'ble Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri. Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both AtJ pathways. After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has now validated bio-ethanol as a feedstock under the EtJ pathway. With this achievement, Praj has further strengthened the versatility and robustness of its feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.

Source: Company Website

+ AXENS CONTINUES ITS PUBLIC CONSULTATION REGARDING ITS CATHODE ACTIVE MATERIAL PRODUCTION PLANT PROJECT

Following the preliminary consultation held from May 5 to July 5, 2025, and the publication of the project owners' report, Axens and RTE (Reseau de Transport d'Electricite - the French Transmission System Operator) have entered into a continuous consultation process to share project-related information with stakeholders and residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut urban communities.

The continuous consultation mechanism implemented by Axens aims to maintain a transparent and constructive dialogue around the project with residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut communities. This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

+ HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. [1 Press Release]

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

Tuskegee University will leverage several of Honeywell's Building Automation solutions. Key features include:

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JJN-SVX3-S0MR-H3WP-00000-00Uber Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:UBER) Posts $10 Billion Robotaxi Push to Challenge Tesla2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:47ZLexisNexisUber Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:UBER) Posts $10 Billion Robotaxi Push to Challenge Tesla

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

11 May 2026 10:04 EDT

Uber Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:UBER) Posts a $10 billion Robotaxi push, allocating $2.5 billion to acquire stakes in existing Robotaxi operators and $7.5 billion to scale its own autonomous fleet. The plan combines equity investments with fleet expansion to leverage partnerships while building internal capacity, aiming to contest the leadership of Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Waymo in the robotaxi market. On 10th May 2026, industry analysts began scrutinizing the strategy as Uber positions itself to compete more aggressively in autonomous mobility. The effort reflects a dual approach that seeks to secure technology through stakes while accelerating deployment through its ride hailing network, which could enhance Uber's scale in urban transportation

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JJP-8M03-RRXR-B37H-00000-00AXENS S.A. - THE RECENT NEWS FROM BASF SE AND OTHER PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKAXENS S.A. - THE RECENT NEWS FROM BASF SE AND OTHER PEERS

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [3 Press Releases]

+ AXENS S.A. A NEW TEXTILE-TO-TEXTILE RECYCLING PROCESS VALIDATED ON AN INDUSTRIAL SCALE

Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN have completed a recycling loop for polyester textiles. Several tens of tons of post-consumer, polyester-rich, European textile wastes, sorted and prepared in France, have been processed in the Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN semi-industrial demonstration unit, located in Japan, to successfully produce the base monomer of a 100% recycled polyester. This step paves the way for circular polyester loops for the textile industry, in particular sportswear, home furnishings and the luxury sector.

Source: Company Website

+ PRAJ DEMONSTRATES INTEGRATED ETHANOL-TO-JET PATHWAY FOR SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL WITH AXENS JETANOL(TRADEMARK) TECHNOLOGY

30 January, 2026 - Praj Industries, a global leader in bioenergy, technology and sustainable solutions, has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) process utilizing Axens JetanolTM technology at its fully Integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre-marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low-carbon aviation fuels. The announcement was made by Mr. Atul Mulay, President - Bionergy at Praj Industries at Wings India 2026, India's premier aviation industry platform in the presence of industry leaders, policymakers, and key stakeholders from the global aviation ecosystem.

The Integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Hon'ble Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri. Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both AtJ pathways. After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has now validated bio-ethanol as a feedstock under the EtJ pathway. With this achievement, Praj has further strengthened the versatility and robustness of its feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.

Source: Company Website

+ AXENS CONTINUES ITS PUBLIC CONSULTATION REGARDING ITS CATHODE ACTIVE MATERIAL PRODUCTION PLANT PROJECT

Following the preliminary consultation held from May 5 to July 5, 2025, and the publication of the project owners' report, Axens and RTE (Reseau de Transport d'Electricite - the French Transmission System Operator) have entered into a continuous consultation process to share project-related information with stakeholders and residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut urban communities.

The continuous consultation mechanism implemented by Axens aims to maintain a transparent and constructive dialogue around the project with residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut communities. This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

(NASDAQ:TSLA) Expands Robotaxi Fleet in Texas as European Demand Surges in France and Sweden2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:47ZLexisNexisTesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Expands Robotaxi Fleet in Texas as European Demand Surges in France and Sweden

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

11 May 2026 10:04 EDT

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) continues to make headlines as it advances its Robotaxi operations and expands sales in Europe. On 11th May 2026, the company deployed more than 20 unsupervised Robotaxis in Austin, with three additional units in Houston and Dallas each, advancing its Texas rollout. The company notes that Waymo remains a leader in the autonomous-vehicle sector, keeping competitive pressure high.

The week also saw European demand rise, with France up 112% in April and Sweden up 111%, while Norway declined 61%, underscoring uneven market dynamics across the region.

Separately, Tesla announced a recall affecting over 218,000 vehicles in the United States, covering the Model S, 3, X, and Y, due to a rearview camera image delay when reversing. Tesla issued a software update to mitigate safety concerns.

Elon Musk's Full Self-Driving initiative drew regulator scrutiny in the European Union over performance in icy conditions, while his $1 trillion pay package came under heightened regulatory focus given safety features. In another development, WattEV placed an order for more than 370 Semi trucks, marking the largest electric vehicle truck order in California history.

Taken together, Tesla is advancing its robotaxi expansion, broadening European sales, addressing safety through recalls and software updates, and pursuing substantial commercial orders, with investors watching regulatory developments and the growth trajectory closely.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

TXNM Energy Inc., a utility holding company operating in the southwestern United States, primarily focuses on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas. As a key player in the energy sector, the company serves residential, commercial, and industrial customers in New Mexico and Texas, emphasizing reliable and sustainable power solutions. Its core activities encompass regulated utility operations, including power plant management and infrastructure maintenance, alongside strategic investments in energy efficiency and grid modernization. The fastest-growing segments of TXNM Energy's business include renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. These initiatives have bolstered the company's portfolio, positioning it for long-term growth amid the transition to a low-carbon economy, while maintaining a strong commitment to operational excellence and customer service. It is the NYSE's 15th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Boyd Gaming Corporation (NYSE:BYD) is a pivotal player in the leisure and hospitality industry, offering a diverse portfolio that includes Boyd Gaming casinos, Boyd Gaming hotels, and Boyd Gaming resorts. The company excels in Boyd Gaming meetings and events, driven by Boyd group sales and Boyd corporate meetings. Key destinations such as Boyd event venues Las Vegas provide extensive Boyd convention space and Boyd MICE services. Customers benefit from Boyd group booking packages and Boyd catering and banquet services, along with the B Connected loyalty program. Core offerings like Boyd slot machines and table games, Boyd poker rooms, and Boyd entertainment and nightlife highlight Boyd hospitality services. Reflecting market dynamics, there is an observed Boyd meeting sales uptick, LVCVA group sales uptick, and Las Vegas corporate meetings increase, notably at Boyd casinos Las Vegas and Boyd regional casinos. Extensive offerings include Boyd hotel meeting rooms, Boyd ballroom rentals, and Boyd corporate event planning. With competitive Boyd venue rental rates and strategic Boyd group travel and Boyd convention bookings, supported by the BYD investor relations group sales, Boyd Gaming NYSE BYD corporate meetings provide valuable options, enhanced by Boyd meeting incentives packages.

December 04 2025: TXNM Energy quarterly dividends up 3.7% sequentially from previous quarter

TXNM Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 42.25c per share for Q4/2025; up 3.7% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 40.75c. The ex-dividend date is Friday, January 30, 2026 and the record date is Friday, January 30, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, February 13, 2026. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended December 04 were $1.63.

Press Release article 3 of 5, 296 words

September 30 2025: TXNM Energy: File SEC Form 10-Q - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-Q

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

The following Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations for TXNM is presented on a combined basis, including certain information applicable to PNM and TNMP. The MD&A for PNM and TNMP is presented as permitted by Form 10-Q General Instruction H(2). This report uses the term "Company" when discussing matters of common applicability to TXNM, PNM, and TNMP. A reference to a "Note" in this Item 2 refers to the accompanying Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements (Unaudited) included in Item 1, unless otherwise specified. Certain of the tables below may not appear visually accurate due to rounding.

MD&A FOR TXNM

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Overview

TXNM is a holding company with two regulated electric utilities, PNM and TNMP, serving approximately 839,000 residential, commercial, and industrial customers in New Mexico and Texas. TXNM strives to create a clean and bright energy future for customers, communities, and shareholders. TXNM's strategy and decision-making are focused on safely providing reliable, affordable, and environmentally responsible power built on a foundation of sustainability.

Recent Developments

Merger

On May 18, 2025, TXNM, Parent, and Merger Sub (both Parent and Merger Sub are affiliates of Blackstone Infrastructure) entered into the Merger Agreement pursuant to which Merger Sub will merge with and into TXNM, with TXNM surviving the Merger as a direct, wholly-owned subsidiary of Parent. Pursuant to the Merger Agreement, each issued and outstanding share of the common stock of TXNM (other than those listed in Note 17) at the Effective Time will be converted into the right to receive $61.25 in cash, without interest.

The proposed Merger has been unanimously approved by the Board.

Press Release article 4 of 5, Source: WEC, 78 words

September 25 2025: TXNM Energy quarterly dividends steady

TXNM Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 40.75c per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q2/2025) dividend of 40.75c. The ex-dividend date is Friday, October 24, 2025 and the record date is Friday, October 24, 2025 and it is payable on Friday, November 14. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended September 25 were $1.61.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: Company Website, 317 words

August 28 2025: TXNM Energy Shareholders Overwhelmingly Approve Acquisition by Blackstone Infrastructure

TXNM Energy (NYSE: TXNM) shareholders voted overwhelmingly to approve the agreement under which Blackstone Infrastructure will acquire TXNM Energy at a special shareholders meeting held earlier today. Under the terms of the proposed agreement, TXNM Energy shareholders will receive $61.25 in cash for each share of TXNM Energy common stock held at closing.

Of the shares voted, 99.6 percent voted to approve the proposed agreement, reflecting 88.2 percent of shares issued and outstanding on the record date. TXNM Energy will report the final vote results in a Current Report on Form 8-K to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Regulatory approvals from the New Mexico Public Regulation Commission, Public Utility Commission of Texas, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, Department of Justice (Hart Scott-Rodino Clearance), Nuclear Regulatory Commission and Federal Communications Commission are being pursued.

TXNM Energy continues to anticipate that the closing of the acquisition will occur in the second half of 2026, subject to the satisfaction or waiver of the customary closing conditions, including among other things, receipt of required state and federal regulatory approvals.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DALLAS, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Energy Transfer LP has shown some strength in recent days. Energy Transfer LP (NYSE:ET), the NYSE's 5th largest Pipeline company by market cap, increased 0.4% during its previous trading session to close at $20.10.

Energy Transfer LP (NYSE:ET) is a leading midstream energy company specializing in natural gas, crude oil, and refined product transportation and storage across North America. With a vast network of pipelines and strategically located terminals, Energy Transfer facilitates efficient energy distribution nationwide. The company's advanced control systems and integration of cutting-edge technologies enhance operational efficiency, while their continued expansion into renewable energy reflects a commitment to sustainability and innovation. Through its scale, diversified asset base, and robust infrastructure, Energy Transfer maintains a competitive edge, supporting long-term growth and stability in the ever-evolving energy sector.

Wed May 13, 2026: Energy Transfer's Growth Traced to Kelcy Warren's Leadership Legacy

Energy Transfer LP ranks among the largest publicly traded energy companies in the United States, moving natural gas, natural gas liquids, crude oil, and refined products through a network that spans the country. On 13th May 2026, it became clear that this scale didn't happen by accident. The executive team behind Energy Transfer reflects decades of accumulated experience, and the culture traces directly back to the company's founder.

Kelcy Warren co-founded Energy Transfer in 1996 with roughly 200 miles of natural gas pipeline in East Texas. Since then, it's grown into a national midstream enterprise handling natural gas, natural gas liquids, crude oil, and refined products. As Executive Chairman and Chairman of the Board, Warren has been in the energy sector for more than 45 years, earning accolades that include membership in the Horatio Alger Association of Distinguished Americans and induction into the Texas Business Hall of Fame. His philanthropic efforts, including a $12 million donation to the University of Texas-Arlington, underscore his commitment to community engagement.

Day-to-day operations at Energy Transfer fall to co-CEOs Tom Long and Mackie McCrea, both of whom have spent years learning the intricacies of the company. Long joined through the 2015 merger with Regency GP LLC and has held various positions, including Group CFO. McCrea's tenure at Energy Transfer dates back to 1997, and he has advanced through several key roles leading to his current position as co-CEO. Their deep-rooted experience reflects a managerial model designed to effectively handle the scale of the operations.

The company's institutional depth extends into its finance and legal functions, which are led by long-standing executives. Dylan Bramhall, Group Chief Financial Officer, has been with Energy Transfer since 2006 and oversees critical financial operations. Jim Wright, Executive Vice President, General Counsel, and Chief Compliance Officer, joined the company's legal team in 2005. The longevity and expertise of these leaders contribute to the organizational stability and strategic direction of Energy Transfer.

The consistency across the executive bench at Energy Transfer is indicative of the organizational culture that Kelcy Warren has built over nearly three decades. This leadership framework has positioned Energy Transfer as a major force in U.S. energy infrastructure, reflecting not only the company's growth but also its commitment to excellence in the energy sector.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 296 words

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Energy Transfer LP Boosts Quarterly Cash Distribution, Sparking Investor Interest

Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET) has announced an increase in its quarterly cash distribution to $0.3375 per common unit, amounting to an annualized rate of $1.35. This announcement comes as part of the company's first-quarter results for the year, which ended on the 31st of March, 2026.

This new distribution rate represents an increase of over 3 percent compared to the same quarter in the previous year, highlighting Energy Transfer's commitment to returning value to its unitholders. The distribution will be paid on the 20th of May, 2026, to unitholders of record as of the close of business on the 8th of May 2026.

In conjunction with this announcement, Energy Transfer LP has scheduled the release of its earnings report for the first quarter of 2026 on the 5th of May 2026, before the market opens. A conference call will follow at 8:00 a.m. Central Time, where the company's management will discuss the quarterly results and provide updates on its operations. Investors and analysts can access the call via a live webcast on the Energy Transfer website.

Energy Transfer LP stands as one of the largest operators in the energy sector in the United States, boasting a portfolio that includes approximately 140,000 miles of pipeline and related infrastructure. The company's operations span across 44 states and encompass various segments, including natural gas, crude oil, and natural gas liquids. This diversification positions Energy Transfer favorably in the market, allowing it to capitalize on growth opportunities within the energy landscape.

As the company continues to expand its operational capabilities, investors are keenly watching for further updates on distribution levels and overall company performance. Energy Transfer's strong cash distribution growth is expected to generate interest among income-seeking investors, reinforcing its reputation as a key player in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE AND INDEPENDENT RATINGS

5.1 Activities

Energy Transfer LP is a leading master limited partnership traded on the New York Stock Exchange, focused on midstream energy infrastructure in the United States. The company primarily engages in the gathering, processing, transportation, and storage of natural gas, natural gas liquids (NGLs), crude oil, and refined products through an extensive network of pipelines, terminals, and storage facilities. This integrated approach supports energy producers and consumers, enhancing supply chain efficiency across domestic and international markets. Among its fastest-growing segments are NGL fractionation and logistics, fueled by increasing demand from the petrochemical sector and export opportunities, as well as expansions in crude oil transportation infrastructure. Additionally, Energy Transfer is investing in renewable natural gas and low-carbon initiatives, positioning it for sustainable growth in a transitioning energy industry. With a strong operational footprint, the company plays a vital role in North America's energy supply. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

Energy Transfer LP (NYSE:ET) is a leading natural gas pipeline operator offering comprehensive midstream services including NGL pipeline services, crude oil transportation, and storage terminals. Recently, Energy Transfer LP announced an increase in its quarterly cash distribution, underlining its commitment to enhancing shareholder value through a boosted dividend policy. The Energy Transfer cash distribution per unit, including details like the distribution record date and payable date, was officially revealed in their press release distribution increase, demonstrating financial robustness and shareholder focus. Energy Transfer’s distributable cash flow (DCF) and distribution coverage ratio further highlight its strategic positioning in the market as it continues to deliver steady growth in Energy Transfer midstream distributions. With an expanded footprint in LPG distribution and a solid track record, investors can expect a consistent return from Energy Transfer's distribution yield, reinforced by diligent investor relations and a positive payout update for stakeholders.
